--get reprts_to_key for known reports_to's
update p 
set reports_to_key = l.position_key
from hr.dim_position p inner join hr.dim_position l
on p.reports_to = l.position_nbr
go
--assign missing reports_to_keys
update hr.dim_position
set reports_to_key = 0 where ((reports_to is null or reports_to ='') and reports_to_key is null)
go
--assign unknown reports_to_key
update hr.dim_position
set reports_to_key = -1 where (reports_to is not null AND reports_to <>'' and reports_to_key is null)
go

-- manual emails: from lkp_active_directory
UPDATE e
SET e.email = lkp_ad.ad_email
    , e.email_source = 'Manual'
--select *
FROM hr.dim_employee e
INNER JOIN [ps_hcm].[lkp_active_directory] lkp_ad
    ON e.first_name + '-' + e.last_name = lkp_ad.f_l_name
WHERE e.email IS NULL                               -- employee doesn't have an email match yet
    AND lkp_ad.ad_email IS NOT NULL                 -- has email in lkp_active_directory
    AND e.first_name + '-' + e.last_name NOT IN     -- name in lkp_active_directory does not link to more than 1 email
                                        (
                                            SELECT f_l_name
                                            FROM [ps_hcm].[lkp_active_directory] lkp_ad0
                                            WHERE f_l_name IS NOT NULL
                                                AND ad_email IS NOT NULL
                                            GROUP BY f_l_name
                                            HAVING COUNT(1) > 1
                                        )
    AND e.first_name + '-' + e.last_name NOT IN     -- exclude employees having similar first + last name combinations
                                (
                                    SELECT first_name + '-' + last_name
                                    FROM hr.dim_employee
                                    GROUP BY first_name, last_name
                                    HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
                                )
    AND lkp_ad.ad_email NOT IN                      -- email in lkp_active_directory has not been matched to another employee
                                (
                                    SELECT email
                                    FROM hr.dim_employee
                                    WHERE email IS NOT NULL
                                )

/* -- If an employee is marked to have any disabled email account through MyID, 
      then remove their email in dim_employee that are not populated from FIM source (other sources are likely to contain disabled email).
   -- This is a workaround to address issue from HRIS-2574/HRIS-2631/HRIS-2634 after the new MyID data feed structure is in place, 
      as we don't have disabled email address info any more (info used to be provided in SHARED_HA.ad.HR_PrimaryJobs).
   -- Occasionally there are mismatches of employee ID between dim_employee and MyID (they do have a valid email from FIM but not being populated due to different ID), 
      therefore email address need to be double checked against the ranked look up table before removing.
   -- If an employee reach out that they don't see their valid email being populated in dim_employee,
      may suggest them to request MyID to add the valid email into FIM source */
UPDATE a
SET email = NULL,
	email_source = NULL
FROM hr.dim_employee AS a
INNER JOIN ps_hcm.Outbound_HRIS_AD_Email_Addresses_Summary_With_Disabled AS b ON a.emplid = b.emplid 
LEFT JOIN ps_hcm.lkp_fim_emplid_email AS c ON a.email = c.email
WHERE ISNULL(a.email_source, '') <> 'FIM' AND b.email_status = 'disabled' AND b.job_ha = 'PHSA' AND c.email IS NULL

--2017-11-20: Add 3 more flags - 
		----Flg Frst Dat Curr Fy Period
		----Flg Frst Dat Curr Fy Prior Period
		----Flg Frst Dat Prior Fy Same Curr Period

-- use STG_PUBLISH
if OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#result') is not null drop table #result
;WITH vars 
	(current_fy,prior_fy,current_fp,prior_fp,prior_fp_fy,current_fp_end_date,prior_fp_fy_start_date,as_of_date)
AS 
	(select
		p.current_fy
		,p.prior_fy
		,p.current_fp
		,d.fiscal_period prior_fp
		,d.fiscal_year prior_fp_fy
		,p.current_fp_end_date
		,ds.date_key prior_fp_fy_start_date
		,(select max(as_of_date_key) from hr.fact_job_snapshot) as_of_date
	from 
		shared.dim_date d (nolock)
	left outer join		
		(select 
			c.current_fy
			,dp.fiscal_year prior_fy
			,c.current_fp
			,c.current_fp_end_date
		from 
			shared.dim_date dp 
		left outer join	
			(select 
				dc.fiscal_year current_fy
				,dc.fiscal_period current_fp
				,dc.date_key current_fp_end_date
			from 
				shared.dim_date dc (nolock)
			where 
				dc.date_key = (select 
								max(dc1.date_key) 
							from
								shared.dim_date dc1 (nolock)
							where
								dc1.fiscal_period <> (select dc2.fiscal_period from shared.dim_date dc2 where dc2.date_key = convert(int,convert(varchar(8),getdate(),112)))
								and dc1.date_key <= convert(int,convert(varchar(8),getdate(),112)))) c
			on 1=1					
		where
			dp.date_key = (select
							max(dp1.date_key)
						from
							shared.dim_date dp1 (nolock)
						where
							dp1.fiscal_year <> c.current_fy
							and dp1.date_key < c.current_fp_end_date)) p	
		on 1=1		
	left outer join
		shared.dim_date ds (nolock)
		on ds.date_key = (select
							min(ds2.date_key)
						from
							shared.dim_date ds2 (nolock)
						where
							ds2.fiscal_year = p.prior_fy
							and ds2.fiscal_period = p.current_fp)														
	where 
		d.date_key = (select 
						max(d1.date_key) 
					from
						shared.dim_date d1 (nolock)
					where
						d1.fiscal_period <> p.current_fp
						and d1.date_key <= p.current_fp_end_date)) 	


select  
	--d.*	,
	d.date_key
	
	,case when d.fiscal_year = v.current_fy and d.fiscal_period = v.current_fp then 1 else 0 end flg_curr_fy_period
	,case when d.fiscal_year = v.prior_fp_fy and d.fiscal_period = v.prior_fp then 1 else 0 end flg_curr_fy_prior_period
	,case when d.fiscal_year = v.prior_fy and d.fiscal_period = v.current_fp then 1 else 0 end flg_prior_fy_same_curr_period
	,case when d.date_key between v.prior_fp_fy_start_date and v.current_fp_end_date then 1 else 0 end flg_prior_14_fy_period
	,case when d.date_key = v.as_of_date then 1 else 0 end as_of_date
	--,'' [Current and Prior 2 Fiscal Years]	 
into #result
from 
	shared.dim_date d
left outer join
	vars v
	on 1=1


	select *
	from #result



truncate table hr.dim_date_flag
insert into hr.dim_date_flag
(date_key, flg_curr_fy_period , flg_curr_fy_prior_period , flg_prior_fy_same_curr_period , flg_prior_14_fy_period , as_of_date, 
flg_frst_dat_curr_fy_period, flg_Lst_dat_curr_fy_period, 
flg_frst_dat_curr_fy_prior_period, flg_lst_dat_curr_fy_prior_period , 
flg_frst_dat_prior_fy_same_curr_period, flg_lst_dat_prior_fy_same_curr_period)


select r.*, 
isnull(fcp.date_key, 0 ) as flg_Frst_dat_curr_fy_period, isnull(cp.date_key, 0) as flg_Lst_dat_curr_fy_period, 
isnull(fcpp.date_key, 0 ) as flg_Frst_dat_curr_fy_prior_period, isnull(cpp.date_key, 0) as flg_lst_dat_curr_fy_prior_period, 
isnull(fpcp.date_key, 0 ) as flg_Frst_dat_prior_fy_same_curr_period, isnull(pcp.date_key,0) as flg_lst_dat_prior_fy_same_curr_period
from #result as r 

left join 
(select  max(date_key) as date_key
from #result
where flg_curr_fy_period = 1
) as cp--flg_Lst_day_curr_fy_period
on r.date_key = cp.date_key
left join 
(
select  max(date_key) as date_key
from #result
where flg_curr_fy_prior_period = 1
) as cpp
on r.date_key = cpp.date_key
left join 
(
select   max(date_key) as date_key
from #result
where flg_prior_fy_same_curr_period = 1
) as pcp
on r.date_key = pcp.date_key
left join
(select min(date_key) as date_key
from #result
where flg_curr_fy_period = 1
) as fcp--flg_frst_day_curr_fy_period
on r.date_key = fcp.date_key
left join 
(
select min(date_key) as  date_key
from #result
where flg_curr_fy_prior_period = 1
) as fcpp
on r.date_key = fcpp.date_key
left join 
(
select  min(date_key)  as date_key
from #result
where flg_prior_fy_same_curr_period = 1
) as fpcp
on r.date_key = fpcp.date_key


where flg_curr_fy_period + flg_curr_fy_prior_period + flg_prior_fy_same_curr_period + flg_prior_14_fy_period +  as_of_date > 0
order by date_key

SET NOCOUNT ON
IF OBJECT_ID('hr.tmp_cte_report_to') IS NOT NULL
 DROP TABLE hr.tmp_cte_report_to;
GO 
WITH cte_report_to (position_nbr,chain,level_nbr)
AS
(
	select distinct M.position_nbr,cast('' as varchar(2000)) as chain,0 as level_nbr
	from hr.dim_position L
	 inner join hr.dim_position M
	 on L.reports_to = M.position_nbr
	 inner join hr.dim_position R
	 on M.reports_to = R.position_nbr
	 where isnull(L.reports_to,'') <> '' and isnull(M.reports_to,'') <> ''
 UNION ALL
	 select B.position_nbr,cast(B.position_nbr+case when LEN(A.chain) > 0 THEN ','+ A.chain else '' END as varchar(2000)) as chain,A.level_nbr+1
	 from cte_report_to A
	  inner join hr.dim_position B
	  on A.position_nbr = B.reports_to and CHARINDEX(B.position_nbr,A.chain) <=0
	 WHERE A.position_nbr <> B.position_nbr and A.level_nbr < 15
 )
 SELECT T2.position_nbr,T2.effective_from as effdt,T2.business_unit,T2.job_code as jobcode,T2.reports_to
 INTO hr.tmp_cte_report_to
 FROM cte_report_to T1
   INNER JOIN hr.dim_position T2
	ON T1.position_nbr = T2.reports_to and CHARINDEX(T2.position_nbr,T1.chain) >0;
GO
IF EXISTS (SELECT TOP 1 * FROM hr.tmp_cte_report_to)
BEGIN

 UPDATE T2
 SET T2.reports_to_key = -1
 FROM hr.tmp_cte_report_to T1
   INNER JOIN hr.dim_position T2
 ON T1.position_nbr = T2.position_nbr

DECLARE	@str_query as nvarchar(2000)

SET @str_query = N'SELECT position_nbr, effdt, business_unit, jobcode FROM hr.tmp_cte_report_to '
				+N' ORDER BY position_nbr'

/*
Oct 6, 2010: Intial implementation was simply putting effective _to date from the next row in sequence for the same emplid and same empl_rcd
			This update goes step further so that each job record will have reference to it's previous row and to next row, for the same emplid and empl_rcd
			The objective with this is to ease job transation reporting 9status changes, department changes etc)
			zero = previous row
			one = current row
			two = next row 
--assign effective_to date
--two steps process
--1: order job records by emplid, empl_rcd, effective_from, effseq. assign current row effective_to = next row' effective from for the same emplid and empl_rcd
*/
UPDATE j
SET    effective_to = lkp.effective_to, previous_job_key = lkp.prev_job_key, next_job_key=lkp.next_job_key
FROM   hr.dim_job j
       INNER JOIN (SELECT one.job_key,
                          one.emplid,
                          one.empl_rcd,
                          one.effective_from,
                          two.effective_from AS effective_to,
                          one.r,
                          two.r2,
                          zero.r0,
                          zero.job_key as prev_job_key,
                          two.job_key as next_job_key
                   FROM   (SELECT job_key,
                                  emplid,
                                  empl_rcd,
                                  effective_from,
                                  effseq,
                                  employment_status,
                                  r=Dense_rank() OVER (PARTITION BY emplid, empl_rcd ORDER BY effective_from, effseq)
                           FROM   hr.dim_job) one
                          left outer JOIN (SELECT job_key,
                                             emplid,
                                             empl_rcd,
                                             effective_from,
                                             effseq,
                                             employment_status,
                                             r2=Dense_rank() OVER (PARTITION BY emplid, empl_rcd ORDER BY effective_from, effseq) - 1
                                      FROM   hr.dim_job) two
                                ON one.emplid = two.emplid
                               AND one.empl_rcd = two.empl_rcd
                               AND one.r = two.r2
                          left outer JOIN (SELECT job_key,
                                             emplid,
                                             empl_rcd,
                                             effective_from,
                                             effseq,
                                             employment_status,
                                             r0=Dense_rank() OVER (PARTITION BY emplid, empl_rcd ORDER BY effective_from, effseq) + 1
                                      FROM   hr.dim_job) zero            
                            ON one.emplid = zero.emplid
                               AND one.empl_rcd = zero.empl_rcd
                               AND one.r = zero.r0) lkp
         ON j.job_key = lkp.job_key

--step 2:
--set effective to = effective_from for those records who are not considered to be active (A,L,P,S) and not default -1,0 members.
UPDATE hr.dim_job
SET    effective_to = effective_from
WHERE  effective_to IS NULL
       AND employment_status NOT IN ( 'A', 'L', 'P', 'S' )
       AND emplid NOT IN ( '-1', '0' )

select reports_to from hr.dim_job where isnull(effective_to,'') = '' 
and reports_to in (
select distinct reports_to from hr.dim_job 
where isnull(effective_to,'') = ''

declare @original_reports_to varchar(11)
declare @reports_to_key int

 --hard coded

        select top 1 @reports_to_key = job_key

	where reports_to = @original_reports_to

fetch next from pos_cur into @reports_to

/* *********************************************************************************
--Jay Wang Reqeust: 194222
-- if current job is 'P' for job indicator, then current job key will be prev_prim_job_key
--if current job indicator not 'P' then find most recent job record which have job_indicator = 'P'
********************************************************************************** */
/* 
use stg_publish
 */

update j
set j.prev_prim_job_key = isnull(p.prev_prim_job_key, 0)
--select  j.job_indicator, p.prev_prim_job_key, j.*
from hr.dim_job as j

inner join 
(
select sjob.job_indicator , sjob.job_key,
case when sjob.job_indicator = 'p' then sjob.job_key
else pjob.job_key 
end as prev_prim_job_key,
row_number() over (partition by sjob.job_key order by pjob.effective_from desc, pjob.effseq desc) as r

from hr.dim_job as sjob
left join hr.dim_job as pjob
on sjob.job_indicator = 's'
and pjob.emplid = sjob.emplid
and pjob.empl_rcd = sjob.empl_rcd 
and pjob.job_indicator = 'p'
and ((pjob.effective_from < sjob.effective_from)
or (pjob.effective_from = sjob.effective_from and pjob.effseq < sjob.effseq))

) as p
on p.job_key = j.job_key
and p.r=1

/*
Purpose:

Per Zoran's request, build a table that shows flattend Reports To relationship. Zoran will use this table in CUBE for PHC & PHSA

Conditions:
(1) Position data is the source of truth:
(2) Report is based on active/leave employee list
(3) Source of truth for employee list is PS_JOB table
(4) To be included - an employee MUST have a position attached to the job record
(5) Each position MUST have Reports To information in the position data table
(6) ReportsTo information is taken from the Position data (source of truth) and based on job record

Report Specific requirements:

(1) Roll up level is an unknown variable - thus per advice - set up for 12 levels - PHSA actual max is 9 PHC actual max is 6
(2) For each EMPLID - list the next level EMPLID & EMPL_RCD until position reaches the highest in the organization or for the category
(3) Not all positions in an organization rollup to the top position -
(4) PHSA has sevaral positions that do not roll up to the highest position 
(5) Processing starts when loop encounter Position Nbr and Reports To match values


Report Processing:

(1) For each EMPLID - select the position nbr and reports to from temp table
(2) then for each position select the Manager position (reports_to)
(3) then swap each position with manager position - (creating CTE process via recursive processing)
(4) processing stops when Mgr Position Nbr = Reports_to
(5) processing appear solid and logic is sound as far as I can see
(6) to improve performance, created separate variable look up table of PS_POSITION DATA table
(7)

(3) 


Prepared by J.Perera
Date 08 Jul 2014


*/






DECLARE @TempTbl TABLE

(
--ROWNUM INT IDENTITY (1,1) PRIMARY KEY,
ROWNUM INT IDENTITY (1,1),
EMPLID CHAR(6),
EMPL_RCD SMALLINT,
POSITION_NBR NVARCHAR(8),
REPORTS_TO NVARCHAR(8)
PRIMARY KEY (ROWNUM,EMPLID,EMPL_RCD)		-- perhaps a trial error approach - not sure whether this alone improved performance
)

INSERT INTO @TempTbl					-- temp table house all records require processing




SELECT
A.EMPLID,
A.EMPL_RCD,
A.POSITION_NBR,
P.REPORTS_TO

FROM [ps_hcm].[ps_job] A
		
		LEFT OUTER JOIN [ps_hcm].[ps_position_data] P
			ON P.POSITION_NBR = A.POSITION_NBR
				AND P.EFFDT =
					(SELECT MAX(P_ED.EFFDT) FROM [ps_hcm].[ps_position_data] P_ED
						WHERE P_ED.POSITION_NBR = P.POSITION_NBR
							AND P_ED.EFFDT <= GETDATE())
							
					

WHERE A.EFFDT =
		(SELECT MAX(A_ED.EFFDT) FROM [ps_hcm].[ps_job] A_ED
			WHERE A_ED.EMPLID = A.EMPLID
				AND A_ED.EMPL_RCD = A.EMPL_RCD
					AND A_ED.EFFDT <= GETDATE())
					

 AND A.EFFSEQ =
			(SELECT MAX(A_ES.EFFSEQ) FROM [ps_hcm].[ps_job] A_ES
				WHERE A_ES.EMPLID = A.EMPLID
					AND A_ES.EMPL_RCD = A.EMPL_RCD
						AND A_ES.EFFDT = A.EFFDT)
						

AND A.EMPL_STATUS IN ('A','L','P')		-- EE must be in one of these status
AND A.POSITION_NBR <> ''				-- EE must have a position nbr attached to Job Record
AND P.REPORTS_TO <> ''					-- EE must have a reports to in the position data table

*********************************************************************************************************************/

			-- processing begins here by selecting the EMPLID with the lowest @ROWNUM

			SET @ROWNUM = (SELECT MIN(ROWNUM) FROM @TempTbl )
			
							
		WHILE @ROWNUM <= (SELECT MAX(ROWNUM) FROM @TempTbl)
		
		
	BEGIN 
			
			SET @EMPLID = (SELECT EMPLID FROM @TempTbl WHERE ROWNUM = @ROWNUM)
			SET @EMPL_RCD = (SELECT EMPL_RCD FROM @TempTbl WHERE ROWNUM = @ROWNUM)
			SET @POSITION_NBR = (SELECT POSITION_NBR FROM @TempTbl WHERE ROWNUM = @ROWNUM)
			SET @REPORTS_TO = (SELECT REPORTS_TO FROM @TempTbl WHERE ROWNUM = @ROWNUM)
			
			
			--initialize employee ID, RCD# and roll up lvl
			
			SET @LIST = @EMPLID+','+@EMPL_RCD 
			SET @LVL	= 0
			
		
			--PRINT @LIST
			
			
					-- if posn # = rptsto, it is a top position - CEO (2 or 3 others at PHSA)
		
				     WHILE @POSITION_NBR  <>  @REPORTS_TO 
				       AND @LVL <12
				       
				       
				     
				BEGIN	
				
									
					SET @EE_POSITION_NBR = @POSITION_NBR 
					SET @MGR_POSITION_NBR =  @REPORTS_TO 
				
				

			
				
				SET @MGR_EMPLID = ISNULL((SELECT MIN(P1.EMPLID) FROM [ps_hcm].[ps_posn_incumbents] P1
											WHERE P1.POSITION_NBR  = @MGR_POSITION_NBR 
												AND P1.EMPL_STATUS = 'A'),'Vacant')
				
				--PRINT @MGR_EMPLID - testing only
				
				SET @MGR_EMPL_RCD =		(SELECT MAX(P2.EMPL_RCD) FROM [ps_hcm].[ps_posn_incumbents] P2
												WHERE P2.POSITION_NBR  = @MGR_POSITION_NBR 
													AND P2.EMPLID = @MGR_EMPLID)
				
				
				-- get MgrID & RCD# and attach it to @LIST while loop process						
			
				SET @LIST = @LIST+','+@MGR_EMPLID+','+CAST(@MGR_EMPL_RCD AS NVARCHAR(2))
				
						
				
				SET @LVL = @LVL+1
				
				--PRINT 'LVL'+CAST(@LVL AS NVARCHAR)+' '+'EMPL_RCD'+CAST(@MGR_EMPL_RCD AS NVARCHAR)+@MGR_EMPLID
				
				
				IF @LVL = 1
				
						BEGIN
							SET @EMPLID_LVL1 = @MGR_EMPLID
							SET @EMPL_RCD_LVL1 = @MGR_EMPL_RCD
								
						 END
					
				 IF @LVL = 2
						 
						 BEGIN
							SET @EMPLID_LVL2 = @MGR_EMPLID
							SET @EMPL_RCD_LVL2 = @MGR_EMPL_RCD
								
						  END
		
				 IF @LVL = 3
							 
							BEGIN
								SET @EMPLID_LVL3 = @MGR_EMPLID
								SET @EMPL_RCD_LVL3 = @MGR_EMPL_RCD
								
							 END
							
								
				 IF @LVL = 4
								 
							BEGIN								 
								SET @EMPLID_LVL4 = @MGR_EMPLID
								SET @EMPL_RCD_LVL4 = @MGR_EMPL_RCD
									
							END 
									
									
					 IF @LVL = 5
					 
							BEGIN
							
								SET @EMPLID_LVL5 = @MGR_EMPLID
								SET @EMPL_RCD_LVL5 = @MGR_EMPL_RCD
								
							END
								
						IF @LVL = 6
						 
							BEGIN
									SET @EMPLID_LVL6 = @MGR_EMPLID
									SET @EMPL_RCD_LVL6 = @MGR_EMPL_RCD
									
							END
										
							IF @LVL = 7
								
								BEGIN
								
									SET @EMPLID_LVL7 = @MGR_EMPLID
									SET @EMPL_RCD_LVL7 = @MGR_EMPL_RCD
											
								END		
								
								
							IF @LVL = 8
								
								BEGIN	
							
									SET @EMPLID_LVL8 = @MGR_EMPLID
									SET @EMPL_RCD_LVL8  = @MGR_EMPL_RCD 
									
								 END
									 
													
									
							 IF @LVL = 9
							 
								BEGIN
										SET @EMPLID_LVL9 = @MGR_EMPLID
										SET @EMPL_RCD_LVL9 = @MGR_EMPL_RCD
										
								END
									
									 IF @LVL = 10
									 
								BEGIN
										SET @EMPLID_LVL10 = @MGR_EMPLID
										SET @EMPL_RCD_LVL10 = @MGR_EMPL_RCD
										
								END
								
										 IF @LVL = 11
										 
										 
								BEGIN
											SET @EMPLID_LVL11 = @MGR_EMPLID
											SET @EMPL_RCD_LVL11 =@MGR_EMPL_RCD
								
								END	
								
										 IF @LVL = 12
										 
								BEGIN		 
											SET @EMPLID_LVL12 = @MGR_EMPLID
											SET @EMPL_RCD_LVL12 = @MGR_EMPL_RCD
											
								END	
				 
				 -- after getting MgrID & RCD#, initialize @POSN# with @MgrPosn# (CTE concept) 	
				
					SET @POSITION_NBR = @MGR_POSITION_NBR
									
			
				-- find out RptsTo of the next Posn#
				
					SET @REPORTS_TO =
							(SELECT P3.REPORTS_TO FROM @POSITION_DATA P3
									WHERE P3.POSITION_NBR = @POSITION_NBR)
									
											
			END 		
													
			PRINT CAST(@ROWNUM AS NVARCHAR(10))+' '+'LVL-'+ CAST(@LVL AS NVARCHAR(3))+'  '+@LIST	
			
			INSERT INTO @TblEmpLvl VALUES 
			
		(   --@LVL,
			@EMPLID, 
			  @EMPL_RCD, 
					ISNULL(@EMPLID_LVL1,''), 
					ISNULL(@EMPL_RCD_LVL1,''), 
					
						ISNULL(@EMPLID_LVL2,''),  
						ISNULL(@EMPL_RCD_LVL2,''), 
						
							ISNULL(@EMPLID_LVL3,''), 
							ISNULL(@EMPL_RCD_LVL3,''),
							
								ISNULL(@EMPLID_LVL4,''), 
								ISNULL(@EMPL_RCD_LVL4,''), 
								
									ISNULL(@EMPLID_LVL5,''),  
									ISNULL(@EMPL_RCD_LVL5,''), 
							
							
							
					ISNULL(@EMPLID_LVL6,''),  
					ISNULL(@EMPL_RCD_LVL6,''), 
					
						ISNULL(@EMPLID_LVL7,''),  
						ISNULL(@EMPL_RCD_LVL7,''), 
						
								ISNULL(@EMPLID_LVL8,''),  
								ISNULL(@EMPL_RCD_LVL8,''), 
								
									ISNULL(@EMPLID_LVL9,''), 
									ISNULL(@EMPL_RCD_LVL9,''), 
									
									
							ISNULL(@EMPLID_LVL10,''), 
							ISNULL(@EMPL_RCD_LVL10,''), 
							
								ISNULL(@EMPLID_LVL11,''),  
								ISNULL(@EMPL_RCD_LVL11,''), 
								
									ISNULL(@EMPLID_LVL12,''),  
									ISNULL(@EMPL_RCD_LVL12,'') )
							
												
			-- SET all values to '' before next run			
			
				SET @LVL = 0
	
	
						SET @EMPLID_LVL1 = ''		SET @EMPL_RCD_LVL1 = ''
						SET @EMPLID_LVL2 = ''		SET @EMPL_RCD_LVL2 = ''
						SET @EMPLID_LVL3 = ''		SET @EMPL_RCD_LVL3 = ''
						SET @EMPLID_LVL4 = ''		SET @EMPL_RCD_LVL4 = ''

						SET @EMPLID_LVL6 = ''		SET @EMPL_RCD_LVL6 = ''

						SET @EMPLID_LVL8 = ''		SET @EMPL_RCD_LVL8 = ''
						SET @EMPLID_LVL9 = ''		SET @EMPL_RCD_LVL9 = ''
						SET @EMPLID_LVL10 = ''		SET @EMPL_RCD_LVL10 = ''
						SET @EMPLID_LVL11 = ''		SET @EMPL_RCD_LVL11 = ''
						SET @EMPLID_LVL12 = ''		SET @EMPL_RCD_LVL12 = ''
	
	
					
				SET @ROWNUM = @ROWNUM + 1
						
			END

/*
Requirements:

For the purpose of CAT implementation, it is one of the requirements to find out when a casual employee started to work as a casual. Under normal circumstances 
this would be either the date the casual recd created hired/rehired or set up as additional record.

However, data in the system can in some circumstances be lot more complex than just a clean extract. 
An employee may get hired as regular then terminate and get rehired as casual; or an  employee may have been a casual, 
term, and rehired as cas or a regular transferred to CAS then term and rehired as CAS or a regular employee, transferred to CAS then took some
 form of unpaid leave - this is rare but happening now, or in some other situations CAS ee was on unpaid leave, 
 thus the casual start date for the record has to be after the leave period.

For the purpose of CAT CAS hire date:

(1) it must be the eariest start date for the casual record – excluding :
•	should be after all regular records transactions for the record
•	should be after all leave transactions for the record
•	should be after all termination transactions for the record


** records terminated in error 
The casual start date may show up as after record terminated in error reason - 
however, these are very few situations, and with mimimal impact (fixing it will need 
expanding the code further:

Developed by J.Perera
Date : 02 Feb 2013
Above description updated 26 Sept 2013

*/



SELECT
X.EMPLID,
X.EMPL_RCD,
--X.LABOR_AGREEMENT,
--X.BUSINESS_UNIT,
--X.TERM_CNT,     --- use these fields for troubleshooting in the event of a descrepancy
--X.REG_CNT,
--X.LVE_CNT,

CASE 
     WHEN X.TERM_CNT = 0 AND X.REG_CNT = 0 AND X.LVE_CNT = 0
           THEN CONVERT(CHAR(10),
                     (SELECT MIN(J.EFFDT) FROM [ps_hcm].[ps_job] J
                     WHERE J.EMPLID = X.EMPLID
                           AND J.EMPL_RCD = X.EMPL_RCD
                                AND J.FULL_PART_TIME = 'C'),101) 
                                
                                
           ELSE 
                (SELECT MIN(J.EFFDT) FROM [ps_hcm].[ps_job] J
                     WHERE J.EMPLID = X.EMPLID
                           AND J.EMPL_RCD = X.EMPL_RCD
                           AND J.EMPL_STATUS = 'A'
                                AND J.FULL_PART_TIME = 'C'
                                AND J.EFFDT >=          
                                           (SELECT MAX(J_ED.EFFDT) FROM [ps_hcm].[ps_job] J_ED
                                                WHERE J_ED.EMPLID = J.EMPLID
                                                     AND J_ED.EMPL_RCD = J.EMPL_RCD
                                     
--(1) first check if the recd has had both - change in EMPL_STATUS and FULLPART_TIME  (each count is greater than 0 like EE ID 331029/335907
                                     
                                                           AND ((J_ED.EMPL_STATUS <> 'A'
                                                                AND J_ED.FULL_PART_TIME <> 'C')
                                                                
-- (2) here evaluate records with just one change - either EMPL_STATUS change or FULL_PART_TIME change. both codes get evaluated together - see (( )) ** see notes at the top           
                                                                OR
                                                                     (J_ED.EMPL_STATUS <> 'A'
                                                                          OR J_ED.FULL_PART_TIME <> 'C'))
                                                                           
                                                                     AND J_ED.EFFDT <= X.EFFDT)
                                                                     
                                     AND J.EFFSEQ =
                                                     (SELECT MAX(J_ES.EFFSEQ) FROM [ps_hcm].[ps_job] J_ES
                                                           WHERE J_ES.EMPLID = J.EMPLID
                                                                AND J_ES.EMPL_RCD = J.EMPL_RCD
                                                                     AND J_ES.EFFDT = J.EFFDT)) END AS CAS_START_DT                 
                                
INTO #TMP_CAS_START_DATE
FROM

(
SELECT
A.EMPLID,
A.EMPL_RCD,
A.EMPL_STATUS,
A.REG_TEMP,
A.FULL_PART_TIME,
A.EFFDT,
A.EFFSEQ,
A.BUSINESS_UNIT,
A.LABOR_AGREEMENT,

CASE WHEN 
     (SELECT COUNT(X.EMPL_STATUS) FROM [ps_hcm].[ps_job] X
     WHERE X.EMPLID = A.EMPLID
           AND X.EMPL_RCD = A.EMPL_RCD
                AND X.EMPL_STATUS = 'T'
                     AND X.EFFDT <= A.EFFDT  --- limit the count to recd status as of GETDATE() -if not future dated terms will get counted
                     AND X.EFFSEQ =
                                (SELECT MAX(X_ES.EFFSEQ) FROM [ps_hcm].[ps_job] X_ES
                                     WHERE X_ES.EMPLID = X.EMPLID
                                           AND X_ES.EMPL_RCD = X.EMPL_RCD
                                                AND X_ES.EFFDT = X.EFFDT))>= 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS TERM_CNT, 
                
                
                
CASE WHEN
     (SELECT COUNT(Z.FULL_PART_TIME) FROM [ps_hcm].[ps_job] Z
     WHERE Z.EMPLID = A.EMPLID
           AND Z.EMPL_RCD = A.EMPL_RCD
                AND Z.FULL_PART_TIME <> 'C'
                     AND Z.EFFDT <= A.EFFDT -- limit the count to rcds status as of GETDATE() if not future dated terms will get added

                     AND Z.EFFSEQ =
                                (SELECT MAX(Z_ES.EFFSEQ) FROM [ps_hcm].[ps_job] Z_ES
                                     WHERE Z_ES.EMPLID = Z.EMPLID
                                           AND Z_ES.EMPL_RCD = Z.EMPL_RCD
                                                AND Z_ES.EFFDT = Z.EFFDT))>=1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS REG_CNT, 
                     
                     
                     
CASE WHEN
(SELECT COUNT(X.EMPL_STATUS) FROM [ps_hcm].[ps_job] X
     WHERE X.EMPLID = A.EMPLID
           AND X.EMPL_RCD = A.EMPL_RCD
                AND X.EMPL_STATUS IN ('L','P')
                     AND X.FULL_PART_TIME = 'C'
                     AND X.EFFDT <= A.EFFDT  --- limit the count to recd status as of GETDATE() -if not future dated terms will get counted
                     AND X.EFFSEQ =
                                (SELECT MAX(X_ES.EFFSEQ) FROM [ps_hcm].[ps_job] X_ES
                                     WHERE X_ES.EMPLID = X.EMPLID
                                           AND X_ES.EMPL_RCD = X.EMPL_RCD
                                                AND X_ES.EFFDT = X.EFFDT))>=1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS LVE_CNT
                

FROM [ps_hcm].[ps_job] A

WHERE A.EFFDT =
           (SELECT MAX(A_ED.EFFDT) FROM [ps_hcm].[ps_job] A_ED
                WHERE A_ED.EMPLID = A.EMPLID
                     AND A_ED.EMPL_RCD = A.EMPL_RCD
                           AND A_ED.EFFDT <= GETDATE())    --- limit the count to recd status as of GETDATE()
                           
     AND  A.EFFSEQ =
             (SELECT MAX(A_ES.EFFSEQ) FROM [ps_hcm].[ps_job] A_ES
                WHERE A_ES.EMPLID = A.EMPLID
                     AND A_ES.EMPL_RCD = A.EMPL_RCD
                           AND A_ES.EFFDT = A.EFFDT)
                           
AND A.EMPL_STATUS = 'A'
AND A.FULL_PART_TIME = 'C'
           
           
-- TEST CASES
           
--AND A.EMPLID IN ('351843','000092','000040','000145','000469','000109','000195','000467','000110','002898','003498')     
--AND A.EMPLID IN('002605','002898')
--AND A.EMPLID IN()'334987','334797','331029','335907','002898'

)X

ORDER BY X.EMPLID,X.EMPL_RCD

UPDATE f
SET    casual_start_date_key = d.date_key
FROM   hr.fact_current_job f
       INNER JOIN hr.dim_job j
         ON f.job_key = j.job_key
       INNER JOIN (SELECT emplid,
                          empl_rcd,
                          Min(cas_start_dt) AS casual_start_date
                   FROM   #tmp_cas_start_date t
                   GROUP  BY emplid,
                             empl_rcd) c
         ON j.emplid = c.emplid
            AND j.empl_rcd = c.empl_rcd
       LEFT OUTER JOIN shared.dim_date d
         ON c.casual_start_date = d.DATE

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS #ee_master
SELECT 
 dept.l2name
 , rpt.emplid
 , CASE WHEN jc10.job_code = 'E00031' THEN ee9.name -- job code of ceo at level 10, use the manager name of level 9
        WHEN jc9.job_code = 'E00031' THEN ee8.name
     WHEN jc8.job_code = 'E00031' THEN ee7.name
     WHEN jc7.job_code = 'E00031' THEN ee6.name
     WHEN jc6.job_code = 'E00031' THEN ee5.name
     WHEN jc5.job_code = 'E00031' THEN ee4.name
     WHEN jc4.job_code = 'E00031' THEN ee3.name
     WHEN jc3.job_code = 'E00031' THEN ee2.name
     WHEN jc2.job_code = 'E00031' THEN ee1.name
   END AS evp_or_person_reports_to_CEO_name_ps
 , CASE WHEN jc10.job_code = 'E00031' THEN ee9.first_name+' '+ee9.last_name 
        WHEN jc9.job_code = 'E00031' THEN ee8.first_name+' '+ee8.last_name 
     WHEN jc8.job_code = 'E00031' THEN ee7.first_name+' '+ee7.last_name 
     WHEN jc7.job_code = 'E00031' THEN ee6.first_name+' '+ee6.last_name
     WHEN jc6.job_code = 'E00031' THEN ee5.first_name+' '+ee5.last_name 
     WHEN jc5.job_code = 'E00031' THEN ee4.first_name+' '+ee4.last_name 
     WHEN jc4.job_code = 'E00031' THEN ee3.first_name+' '+ee3.last_name 
     WHEN jc3.job_code = 'E00031' THEN ee2.first_name+' '+ee2.last_name 
     WHEN jc2.job_code = 'E00031' THEN ee1.first_name+' '+ee1.last_name
   END AS evp_or_person_reports_to_CEO
   , CASE WHEN jc10.job_code = 'E00031' THEN rpt.emplid_lvl9 -- job code of ceo at level 10, use the manager name of level 9
        WHEN jc9.job_code = 'E00031' THEN rpt.emplid_lvl8
     WHEN jc8.job_code = 'E00031' THEN rpt.emplid_lvl7
     WHEN jc7.job_code = 'E00031' THEN rpt.emplid_lvl6
     WHEN jc6.job_code = 'E00031' THEN rpt.emplid_lvl5
     WHEN jc5.job_code = 'E00031' THEN rpt.emplid_lvl4
     WHEN jc4.job_code = 'E00031' THEN rpt.emplid_lvl3
     WHEN jc3.job_code = 'E00031' THEN rpt.emplid_lvl2
     WHEN jc2.job_code = 'E00031' THEN rpt.emplid_lvl1
     end as svp_emplid
  ,CASE WHEN jc10.job_code = 'E00031' THEN rpt.empl_rcd_lvl9 -- job code of ceo at level 10, use the manager name of level 9
        WHEN jc9.job_code = 'E00031' THEN rpt.empl_rcd_lvl8
     WHEN jc8.job_code = 'E00031' THEN rpt.empl_rcd_lvl7
     WHEN jc7.job_code = 'E00031' THEN rpt.empl_rcd_lvl6
     WHEN jc6.job_code = 'E00031' THEN rpt.empl_rcd_lvl5
     WHEN jc5.job_code = 'E00031' THEN rpt.empl_rcd_lvl4
     WHEN jc4.job_code = 'E00031' THEN rpt.empl_rcd_lvl3
     WHEN jc3.job_code = 'E00031' THEN rpt.empl_rcd_lvl2
     WHEN jc2.job_code = 'E00031' THEN rpt.empl_rcd_lvl1
  end as svp_empl_rcd

-- Assign the ELT to the L2 name that has the highest # of reports-to
-- global temp table for ssis
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS #ee_per_svp
SELECT 
   m.l2name
   , m.evp_or_person_reports_to_CEO_name_ps
   , m.evp_or_person_reports_to_CEO
 , m.svp_emplid
 , m.svp_empl_rcd
   , COUNT(m.emplid) AS count_of_dir_rpt 
INTO #ee_per_svp
FROM #ee_master AS m
GROUP BY
    m.l2name
 , m.evp_or_person_reports_to_CEO_name_ps
    , m.evp_or_person_reports_to_CEO
 , m.svp_emplid
 , m.svp_empl_rcd
HAVING COUNT(m.emplid) =
       (SELECT MAX(a.cnt)
        FROM (SELECT COUNT(m1.emplid) AS cnt FROM #ee_master AS m1
          WHERE m1.l2name = m.l2name
GROUP BY m1.l2name, m1.evp_or_person_reports_to_CEO) AS a)

--Employees don't report to David Byers directly

SELECT 
  u.empl_id,
  u.empl_rcd,
  u.labor_agreement,
  --u.status,
  CASE WHEN e.name = 'Susan Marie Good' THEN 'Susan Good' WHEN e.name = 'Alexandra Flatt' THEN 'Lexie Flatt'  WHEN e.name = 'Maureen E. O''Donnell' THEN 'Maureen O''Donnell'  WHEN e.name IS NULL  THEN @CEO_Name ELSE e.name END AS svp_name
FROM #unassigned AS u
INNER JOIN hr.rpt_reports_to AS r
  ON u.empl_id = r.emplid 
  AND u.empl_rcd = r.empl_rcd
  AND r.mgr_emplid = @CEO_Id
  AND r.mgr_level NOT IN(0,1)
INNER JOIN hr.rpt_reports_to AS r1
  ON r.emplid = r1.emplid 
  AND r.empl_rcd = r1.empl_rcd
  AND r1.mgr_level = (select max(r2.mgr_level) from [hr].[rpt_reports_to] r2
           where r1.emplid = r2.emplid
            and r1.empl_rcd = r2.empl_rcd
            and r2.mgr_top_flag = 'N'  ---- get the max mgr emplid that is not the top (top being CEO)
              )
LEFT JOIN #EVP AS e  --Include employees who report to Non Employees as second highest leader
  ON r1.mgr_emplid = e.empl_id

UNION

SELECT 
  u.empl_id,

  --u.name,
  u.labor_agreement,

INNER JOIN hr.rpt_reports_to AS r
  ON u.empl_id = r.emplid 
  AND r.empl_rcd = r.empl_rcd
  AND r.mgr_emplid = @CEO_Id
